checks.reason: No stated throughput/QPS/TPS or latency SLO anywhere in docs/README; technical-vision.md explicitly disclaims the concept ('There is no concept of requests per second for a tool a human runs by hand or a script calls once per file'). Dimension is informational for system_type=cli-tool per rubric_manifest.json's system_type_overrides, so this absence is not counted as a risk.

No growth-horizon or expected-scale-in-N-years figure documented anywhere; technical-vision.md explicitly frames growth as 'more file formats or flags,' not load growth. Informational for cli-tool — absence not penalized.

No scale target exists to carry forward from (a)/(b); scalability.a below is calibrated accordingly ('no throughput ceiling is a meaningful concept for a single-invocation process'), consistent with cli-tool's informational status for this whole dimension.

evidence.note: main() has zero I/O beyond one file read, zero background execution, zero cross-invocation state
stateless static class, matches vision's 'no persistence, no schema' claim

checks.reason: The only architecture decision present in this single-commit repo (the initial implementation) is fully consistent with the vision's stated no-server/no-persistence stance. Noted limitation: repo has only 1 commit total, so there are not literally '2-3 recently-made decisions' to check independently — this is a single data point, not three.
No ORM, no @Id/entity mapping, no persisted identity of any kind exists — the tool holds no state between invocations and defines no entities.
No columns/tables/fields to categorize for naming-convention drift — the only data is a transient in-memory String per invocation.
No DDL files exist anywhere under this directory (find for *.sql/*ddl* returned nothing), so there is nothing to grep FOREIGN KEY/REFERENCES against.
No partitioning/sharding of any kind — no persistent storage exists at all.

No currency/monetary or numeric persisted data exists; JsonFormatter treats all input as opaque characters and never parses numeric literals at all (confirmed reconciliation finding f3), so there is no decimal-vs-float choice to evaluate.
There is no schema to check forward-readiness of. The vision's stated 'coming next' (more formats/flags, technical-vision.md line 13) is a code-level extension point, not a schema-evolution question.
No tables exist at all, columnar or otherwise, so there is nothing for a JSON-blob shadow representation to duplicate.

Grepped both source files for hardcoded thread/worker/concurrency/timeout/batch-size numbers — none exist; the process handles exactly one file per invocation by design, single-threaded, with no config surface at all. Dimension is informational for cli-tool — no ceiling concept applies to a single-shot process.

No external calls exist anywhere in the 2-file source tree (no network, DB, or third-party API calls) — the only I/O is one local Files.readString call per invocation. No cost/rate guardrail dimension applies.

Only one behavior/extension point exists currently (JSON pretty-print); with just 2 files and one code path, there is no second conceptually-identical concern to compare modeling consistency against yet.

No traffic/errors/latency/saturation signals exist anywhere (no metrics library, no counters, no timers) — expected for a single-shot local process with no operator watching it in real time. Dimension is informational for cli-tool per system_type_overrides.

No retry/recovery/reconciliation code path exists to trace — on failure the process exits non-zero (uncaught IOException, or an explicit exit(2) for bad usage in JsonFormatterCli.java:16) with no persistent/queued state to recover, so there is no 'drop and recover later' pattern for this check to apply to. Informational for cli-tool.

evidence.note: bus_factor_hotspots: [], high_diversity_hotspots: [] — repo has 1 total commit, single author (Sumeet Sharma), below compute_churn.py's hotspot-min-commits threshold

checks.reason: No bus-factor hotspot signal fires because the repo is a single fresh commit; sanity-checked with the raw churn.json output rather than treating the empty hotspot lists as evidence of health on their own. This is a genuine limitation of the signal (too young to show a pattern), not a clean bill of health in the mature-codebase sense — noted explicitly rather than silently treated as 'no risk.'

extensibility.a (f8) found no pattern doc exists, but since there is exactly one implemented instance of the extension point (no second format/flag yet), no inconsistency cost has actually been paid yet — this check evaluates present cost of inconsistent adoption, which requires at least two instances to compare.
